sources.title: On the motion of a viscous liquid filling space
sources.notes: Foundational global weak-solution work; the theorem is from the 1934 original.

sources.title: Partial regularity of suitable weak solutions of the Navier-Stokes equations
sources.notes: Classical partial-regularity theorem for suitable weak solutions.

sources.title: L3,infinity-solutions of Navier-Stokes equations and backward uniqueness
sources.notes: Velocity L-infinity in time, L3 in space endpoint regularity.

sources.title: Direction of Vorticity and the Problem of Global Regularity for the Navier-Stokes Equations
sources.notes: Foundational geometric depletion criterion.

sources.title: On Leray's self-similar solutions of the Navier-Stokes equations
sources.notes: Excludes an integrable class of backward self-similar profiles.

sources.title: Leray's Self-Similar Solutions to the Navier-Stokes Equations with Profiles in Marcinkiewicz and Morrey Spaces
sources.version: SIAM J. Math. Anal. 50(1), 541-556; matching arXiv:1509.08177
sources.notes: Theorem 1.3 says a weak Leray profile in W1,2 locally and L(q,infinity), for 12/5 less than q less than 6, is zero. In particular it excludes nonzero continuously backward self-similar profiles in weak L3.

sources.title: The Navier-Stokes equations in nonendpoint borderline Lorentz spaces
sources.notes: Regularity for L-infinity in time, L(3,q) in space with finite q.

sources.title: Finite time blowup for an averaged three-dimensional Navier-Stokes equation
sources.notes: Blow-up is for an averaged nonlinearity, not the true Navier-Stokes equation.

sources.title: Nonuniqueness of weak solutions to the Navier-Stokes equation
sources.notes: Finite-energy weak nonuniqueness; not a smooth-data blow-up theorem.

sources.title: Non-uniqueness of Leray solutions of the forced Navier-Stokes equations
sources.notes: Two Leray solutions with zero initial velocity and the same force.

sources.title: Local-in-space estimates near initial time for weak solutions and forward self-similar solutions
sources.notes: Forward self-similar solutions from minus-one-homogeneous initial data.

sources.title: Global regularity for the hyperdissipative Navier-Stokes equation below the critical order
sources.notes: Part of the dissipation-strength model ladder near exponent 5/4.

sources.title: Extension Theorems for BMO
sources.notes: Homogeneous BMO extension theorem used in O2607-05; the ball extension constant is scale-invariant.

sources.title: Factorization Theorems for Hardy Spaces in Several Variables
sources.notes: Strong Lp commutator estimate used at the two interpolation endpoints in O2607-06.

sources.title: On L(p,q) Spaces
sources.notes: Lorentz-space interpolation source; O2607-06 uses explicit real interpolation rather than reflexivity.

sources.title: On Functions of Bounded Mean Oscillation
sources.notes: Higher local integrability of BMO oscillations used at exponents 2 and 4 in O2607-07.

sources.title: Navier-Stokes Equations and Area of Interfaces
sources.notes: Source for the scalar vorticity-magnitude inequality reconstructed by Kato regularisation in O2607-08.

sources.title: Sulla diseguaglianza di Sobolev in spazi di Lorentz
sources.notes: Sobolev-Lorentz embedding source used in the O2607-09 energy reconstruction.

sources.title: Convolution Operators and L(p,q) Spaces
sources.notes: Rearrangement convolution inequality used with the exact Riesz-kernel rearrangement in O2607-12.

sources.title: A Geometric Measure-Type Regularity Criterion for Solutions to the 3D Navier-Stokes Equations
sources.notes: Published sparseness and harmonic-measure regularity criterion used as the structural source for O2607-13 through O2607-16.

sources.title: Smoothness of Koch-Tataru Solutions to the Navier-Stokes Equations Revisited
sources.notes: Local L-infinity mild solvability and spatial analyticity input used in the repaired O2607-14 time selection.

sources.title: Ordering of Sets, Hyperbolic Metric, and Harmonic Measure
sources.version: J. Math. Sci. 95(3), 2256-2266 (1999 English translation of 1997 original)
sources.notes: Diameter-set extremal harmonic-measure theorem used with the correctly stated slit disk in O2607-15.

sources.title: Potential Theory in the Complex Plane
sources.version: London Mathematical Society Student Texts 28, Cambridge University Press
sources.notes: Harmonic-measure majorisation principle used on the slit domain in O2607-15.

sources.title: Localized Smoothing for the Navier-Stokes Equations and Concentration of Critical Norms Near Singularities
sources.notes: Published Theorem 2 and equations (14)-(15), matching arXiv:1812.09115v2 pp. 4-5, give every-late-time critical L3 concentration in a parabolic ball centred at each singular point of a first Leray-Hopf blow-up under a scale-critical Morrey Type-I bound.

sources.title: Quantitative Regularity for the Navier-Stokes Equations Via Spatial Concentration
sources.notes: Published Theorem A, equations (9)-(10), gives a logarithmic lower bound for the strong local L3 mass at every radius between the Type-I parabolic scale and a fixed upper radius. Corollary 4.3 gives a finite quantitative bound for the number of terminal singular points under the global weak-L3 Type-I bound. Proposition A.3 gives a quantitative local-energy and pressure window from a uniform unit-ball L2 initial bound.

sources.title: On Stability of Weak Navier-Stokes Solutions with Large L3-Infinity Initial Data
sources.version: Commun. Partial Differential Equations 43(4), 628-651; matching arXiv:1603.03211
sources.notes: Definition 1.1 specifies weak L3-infinity solutions as heat evolution of the initial datum plus an energy-class correction satisfying weak L2 continuity, a correction energy inequality, and the local energy inequality. Theorem 1.3 gives weak-star initial-data stability. The scale-invariant correction estimate depends only on the weak L3 datum bound and elapsed time.

sources.title: A Note on Weak Solutions to the Navier-Stokes Equations That Are Locally in L-Infinity(L3-Infinity)
sources.version: St. Petersburg Math. J. 32(3), 565-576; Algebra i Analiz 32(3), 238-253; matching arXiv:1906.06707
sources.notes: Theorem 1.2 proves that a suitable weak solution with a local-in-space, uniform-in-time weak-L3 bound has only finitely many singular points at the selected terminal time in every compactly contained spatial subdomain. Proposition 1.3 supplies epsilon(M,N) from weak-L3 ceiling M and scale-invariant energy-pressure ceiling N; the proof of Theorem 1.2 takes P=floor(M^3 epsilon^-4)+1 disjoint singular balls and derives a contradiction, so the count is uniform when M and N are uniform. The conclusion is local and does not require global finite energy.

sources.title: On Local Type I Singularities of the Navier-Stokes Equations and Liouville Theorems
sources.version: J. Math. Fluid Mech. 21, article 43; matching arXiv:1811.00502v2
sources.notes: Published Theorem 1.1 characterizes existence of a suitable Type-I singular point by existence of a nontrivial mild bounded ancient solution with finite scale-invariant Type-I quantity. Theorem 1.2 proves that a mild ancient solution with uniformly bounded strong L3 norm along one sequence of times tending to minus infinity is identically zero. Proposition 2.3 is the interior persistence-of-singularities compactness result used to retain a singular point under suitable weak convergence. The stronger Theorem 4.1 uses weak-L3 stability to give a quantitative terminal Besov-distance Liouville criterion. Its displayed blow-down-subspace definition suppresses the critical amplitude factor, while the proof's Navier-Stokes rescaling requires lambda f(lambda dot); independent review validated the repository's proof-consistent repair and found no published erratum. Lemma 2.5 converts weak Serrin control, including uniform weak L3, into the source Type-I bounds for suitable solutions.

sources.title: Logarithmic Depletion of Vortex Stretching and Singularity Evasion in the 3D Navier-Stokes Equations
sources.notes: All sixteen v2 proof obligations are independently closed with documented repairs. Pinned v2 archive SHA-256: 05eb5078deb9c981c7745ba3d084b122b379570e8d770983ed51bc9c371e7488.

sources.title: Nonuniqueness of Leray-Hopf solutions to the unforced incompressible 3D Navier-Stokes Equation
sources.notes: Computer-assisted singular-initial-data result; independent reproduction pending.
